free_text: Elastic scattering of alpha particles by O16.
Natural oxygen gas was contained in the gas cell at a
pressure of about 3-cm Hg. The window of the gas cell
was 9 micro meters thick aluminium foil.
The beam energy was determined by comparing the
excitation curve of carbon-alpha particle elastic
scattering at laboratory angle of 175 degrees with one
measured by the tandem Van de Graaff Accelerator at
University of Minnesota. In the course of measurements
there was monitored the beam energy by proton resonance
signal from a probe in the analyzer magnet.
Two or three silicon surface barrier detectors
placed in 10-degree intervals.
110-cm variable energy cyclotron.
